parts.prompt: Identify ONE scientific or technological development that contributed to the Green Revolution.

scoring_points.description: Identify ONE scientific or technological development that contributed to the Green Revolution.

required_elements: Specific scientific or technological development tied to 20th-century agricultural intensification (chemical fertilizers, GMOs, drought-resistant crop varieties, tractors).
alternatives.correct_example: The development of chemical fertilizers was one contribution.

wrong_examples: A vague statement without specific historical evidence.
Citing a social or political development (e.g., land reform).
common_traps: Saying 'better farming' without specifying a technology.
Confusing the Green Revolution with organic farming.
scoring_points.official_rubric: Earns 1 point for identifying ONE scientific or technological development that contributed to the Green Revolution. Examples that earn this point include: the development of chemical fertilizers was one contribution; genetically modified crops and organisms (GMOs) contributed to the Green Revolution; the Green Revolution included new crop varieties that could survive in drier climates; a development was new equipment, such as tractors.

sample_responses.response: The development of chemical fertilizers was one contribution.
sample_responses.commentary: CB-listed example — synthetic nitrogen fertilizers are a defining Green Revolution technology.

sample_responses.response: Genetically modified crops and organisms (GMOs) contributed to the Green Revolution.
sample_responses.commentary: CB-listed example — high-yield hybrid and GMO varieties (Borlaug's dwarf wheat, IR8 rice) are core Green Revolution technologies.

sample_responses.response: The Green Revolution included new crop varieties that could survive in drier climates.
sample_responses.commentary: CB-listed example — drought-resistant strains expanded arable land in the developing world.

parts.prompt: Explain ONE way in which the Green Revolution benefited populations in the developing world.

scoring_points.description: Explain ONE way in which the Green Revolution benefited populations in the developing world.

required_elements: Specific benefit to developing-world populations with causal mechanism (preventing starvation, enabling population growth, freeing labor for other economic activities).
alternatives.correct_example: By providing increased food crops, the Green Revolution prevented starvation and food shortages due to overpopulation.

wrong_examples: A vague statement without specific historical evidence.
Identifying a technology without explaining its benefit.
Citing a benefit that applied only to the developed world.
common_traps: Listing a technology instead of explaining its human benefit.
scoring_points.official_rubric: Earns 1 point for explaining ONE way in which the Green Revolution benefited populations in the developing world. Examples that earn this point include: by providing increased food crops, it prevented starvation and food shortages due to overpopulation; by increasing the availability of crops, it led to population growth; by providing new technologies that increased productivity, the Green Revolution allowed populations to pursue other economic activities.

sample_responses.response: By providing increased food crops, it prevented starvation and food shortages due to overpopulation.
sample_responses.commentary: CB-listed example — explicit mechanism (increased food supply) tied to a specific benefit (preventing famine) in the developing world.

sample_responses.response: By increasing the availability of crops, it led to population growth.
sample_responses.commentary: CB-listed example — links higher yields causally to demographic expansion in places like India and Mexico.

sample_responses.response: By providing new technologies that increased productivity, the Green Revolution allowed populations to pursue other economic activities.
sample_responses.commentary: CB-listed example — explains how labor freed from agriculture enabled economic diversification in the developing world.

parts.prompt: Explain ONE way in which the Green Revolution affected the environment within the developing world.

scoring_points.description: Explain ONE way in which the Green Revolution affected the environment within the developing world.

required_elements: Specific environmental effect with mechanism (fertilizer runoff and pollution, monoculture reducing biodiversity, industrial agriculture creating water/air pollution).
alternatives.correct_example: Increased use of fertilizers sometimes caused chemical runoff or pollution.

wrong_examples: A vague statement without specific historical evidence.
Naming a benefit (belongs in part B) rather than an environmental effect.
Identifying a technology without connecting it to an environmental impact.
common_traps: Naming 'pollution' without identifying the agricultural mechanism.
scoring_points.official_rubric: Earns 1 point for explaining ONE way in which the Green Revolution affected the environment within the developing world. Examples that earn this point include: increased use of fertilizers sometimes caused chemical runoff or pollution; the increasing size of monoculture agriculture limited crop diversity and soil integrity in some cases; the spread of new technologies like tractors led to the industrialization of agriculture, which resulted in water and air pollution.

sample_responses.response: Increased use of fertilizers sometimes caused chemical runoff or pollution.
sample_responses.commentary: CB-listed example — fertilizer-driven nutrient runoff and waterway contamination is a specific environmental consequence tied to the Green Revolution.

sample_responses.response: The increasing size of monoculture agriculture limited crop diversity and soil integrity in some cases.
sample_responses.commentary: CB-listed example — monoculture as a Green Revolution practice, with ecological consequences (biodiversity loss, soil depletion).

sample_responses.response: The spread of new technologies like tractors led to the industrialization of agriculture, which resulted in water and air pollution.
sample_responses.commentary: CB-listed example — ties a specific Green Revolution technology (mechanized equipment) to specific environmental outcomes (air and water pollution).
